title: pleroma glandulosum
intro: Pleroma glandulosum, also known as Plumeria obtusa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a tropical tree native to the Americas, known for its fragrant white, pink, or yellow flowers and its medicinal properties. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Plumeria glandulosum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
intro_study: According to "BioMed Research International", Plectranthus glandulosum (also known as Pleroma glandulosum) may provide potential benefits in the prevention of atherosclerosis due to its inhibition of pancreatic lipase, cholesterol esterase, and lipid uptake, which are key factors in the development of this condition.
